mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals accelerating labor market deterioration, which forces equity markets to price in lower corporate earnings and produces negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in > 55% of triggered months, invalidating the labor deterioration signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer and housing demand, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint decline periods yield positive 60-day returns in > 50% of cases, refuting the synchronized demand collapse mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect immediate supply-side recovery, which generates positive short-term momentum in equity markets over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of months with rising industrial production, breaking the momentum link.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 1.0 percent while the unemployment rate simultaneously rises, it signals a liquidity trap where monetary easing fails to stimulate growth, causing negative equity returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The joint condition produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of observations, disproving the liquidity trap transmission.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month drop in advance retail sales exceeding 2 percent indicates sharp consumer spending withdrawal, which directly reduces near-term corporate profit forecasts and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day returns are positive in > 60% of months meeting the retail sales threshold, invalidating the consumer withdrawal channel.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and rising wage income, which supports corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day returns are negative in > 55% of payroll growth months.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index falls below 215 while industrial production stabilizes, it signals disinflation that removes upward pressure on discount rates, leading to positive equity returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The condition yields negative 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered months, refuting the disinflation discount rate channel.
